content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 10, 1994

Doris A. Wells
515 E. Thelborn Street
Covina, CA 91723

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-172

Dear Ms. Wells:

I am writing as a follow-up to our telephone conversation on
June 6, 1994. As I informed you, the policy of the Legal Division
of the FPPC is that we do not render third party advice, that is,
we only advise the public official or the official's authorized
representative as to the official's duties and obligations under
the Political Reform Act.

Questions regarding the legality of conduct that has already
occurred are referred to our Enforcement Division. If you would
like to pursue your question regarding the councilmember's use of
campaign funds for legal fees, please contact our Enforcement
Division at (916) 322-6441. They will advise you as to whether
there are grounds to file a formal complaint in this matter.

I am enclosing a copy of Government Code Section 89514, which
is the authority for the expenditure of campaign funds for
attorney's fees. Your original letter, No. A-94-172, will be
retained in our files.

I trust this is of assistance to you.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jim Specher
Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

enclosure

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
